Title: and LncRNA modules CcNFYB3-CcMATE35 CcLTCS-CcCS jointly regulate the efflux and synthesis of citrate to enhance aluminium tolerance in pigeon pea

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ in the rhizosphere to reduce aluminium toxicity and improve aluminium tolerance
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aluminium stress; 1 mM or 2 mM AlCl3 at acidic pH
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE family transporter with conserved MATE domain and 12 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by aluminium stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by aluminium stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression; RNAi in combinational lines; subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; citrate excretion assay; root elongation, callose, Al content, Evans blue staining; double-gene transgenics
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citrate efflux from roots and enhances aluminium tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcMATE35 was strongly induced by aluminium stress in pigeon pea roots. 2. CcMATE35 overexpression increased root citrate excretion and alleviated aluminium-induced root injury. 3. Its effect on aluminium tolerance and citrate release was stronger than that of CcMATE25 under +Al conditions. 4. It acts in a regulatory module with CcNFYB3.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target/indicator gene for breeding or engineering aluminium-tolerant pigeon pea and other crops by enhancing citrate efflux
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although core module centers on CcMATE35, discussion suggests possible redundancy with other MATEs and additional upstream regulators independent of CcNFYB3.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ in the rhizosphere to reduce aluminium toxicity and improve aluminium tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increased proportionally with duration of aluminium stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased proportionally with duration of aluminium stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; qRT-PCR; citrate content and citrate release assays; physiological assays under Al stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citrate synthesis, elevates root citrate content and citrate release, and enhances aluminium tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcCS was the TCA-cycle gene whose expression positively tracked aluminium stress duration. 2. Overexpression of CcCS increased citrate content/release and improved aluminium tolerance. 3. RNAi of CcCS caused opposite effects. 4. CcCS functions downstream of lncRNA CcLTCS.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance aluminium tolerance by boosting citrate biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although an enzyme, extracted here as Common_Genes per schema use for non-pathway-transporter/storage roles is not ideal; however CcCS is a core metabolic enzyme and should be considered central to citrate synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Citrate synthesis entry step of TCA cycle
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; committed entry step of citrate formation in TCA cycle
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ in the rhizosphere to alleviate aluminium toxicity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting, but identified as key citrate synthesis determinant under Al stress in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increased with duration of Al stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased with duration of Al stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of citrate in roots and exudates; Al tolerance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases citrate synthesis and supports citrate efflux, thereby enhancing Al tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcCS was the only surveyed TCA-cycle enzyme gene with expression proportional to Al stress duration. 2. CcCS overexpression increased root citrate level, citrate release, chlorophyll and proline under Al stress. 3. CcCS RNAi caused reduced tolerance. 4. CcCS is positively regulated by CcLTCS.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be engineered or selected to improve aluminium tolerance through higher citrate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Root citrate synthesis and export act synergistically; synthesis alone is beneficial but less decisive than efflux according to comparison with CcNFYB3 module.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates CcMATE35 expression by binding its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ in the rhizosphere to improve aluminium tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under Al stress with expression pattern similar to CcMATE35
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under Al stress with expression pattern similar to CcMATE35
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression screening; promoter motif prediction; yeast one-hybrid; promoter fragment mapping; ChIP-PCR; overexpression and RNAi hairy-root lines; double-gene transgenics; citrate excretion and Al tolerance assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances citrate efflux and aluminium tolerance by upregulating CcMATE35
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator connecting Al stress signaling to citrate transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcNFYB3 was identified from Al-induced TFs co-expressed with CcMATE35. 2. It directly bound the CcMATE35 promoter in Y1H and ChIP-PCR assays. 3. Overexpression increased CcMATE35 expression, citrate release, root growth, chlorophyll and proline under Al stress, whereas RNAi caused opposite effects. 4. Combined overexpression with CcMATE35 further enhanced Al tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for breeding/engineering Al-tolerant plants via activation of citrate efflux machinery
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May regulate additional Al-tolerance genes beyond CcMATE35, potentially causing broader transcriptional effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests CcNFYB3 regulates additional Al-related genes besides CcMATE35 and may partly compensate for CcMATE35 loss.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation; exact mechanism unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ in the rhizosphere to improve aluminium tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increased consistently over time under Al stress; pattern comparable to CcCS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased consistently over time under Al stress; pattern comparable to CcCS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: lncRNA transcriptome analysis; target prediction by genomic proximity; coding-potential analysis; overexpression and RNAi hairy-root lines; qRT-PCR of CcCS; citrate content/release assays; Al tolerance phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CcCS expression, increases citrate synthesis and root citrate accumulation/release, and enhances aluminium tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive noncoding regulator of citrate synthesis module under Al stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcLTCS was identified as an Al-induced lncRNA candidate targeting CcCS. 2. Its expression tracked Al stress duration and paralleled CcCS expression. 3. Overexpression upregulated CcCS and improved Al tolerance, while RNAi reduced tolerance. 4. Together with CcNFYB3, it synergistically enhances aluminium tolerance by balancing citrate synthesis and efflux.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or engineering target for improving aluminium tolerance through enhanced citrate biosynthesis, though regulatory mechanism should be clarified first
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mechanism unresolved; possible broader RNA-mediated regulatory effects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not established; authors hypothesize possible interaction with protein complexes or transcription factors
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alternative spliced isoform CcLTCS.1 lacks protein-coding potential. Mechanism remains speculative and may involve chromatin regulation, TF interaction, or RNA-RNA pairing.
